{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2eb534b6c8ed40089d193154","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp\n2\t\n3\t**A universal personal interface. Speak or type stream-of-consciousness input across any domain — projects, research, lookups, personal context — and receive parallel, organized results on a live canvas or in audio.**\n4\t\n5\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## The Problem\n10\t\n11\tManaging multiple active projects means constant context-switching: open the right tab, re-establish context, ask the question, wait, switch to the next project, repeat. The input channel — typing into a specific session — is also the routing layer, and that conflation is the friction.\n12\t\n13\tBeyond projects: the same friction applies to research, personal notes, lookups, reminders, and anything else that requires knowing where to ask. You shouldn't have to know where to ask.\n14\t\n15\t---\n16\t\n17\t## What aide-de-camp Does\n18\t\n19\tYou speak or type without caring which project, domain, or system you're addressing:\n20\t\n21\t> \"Has the options pipeline caught up? Also what's the state of the IBKR MCP, and remind me what name candidates we had for pdftract.\"\n22\t\n23\taide-de-camp:\n24\t1. Segments the utterance into intent threads and tags each with project and intent type\n25\t2. Dispatches each thread to a parallel agent — one per thread, all running concurrently\n26\t3. Each agent fetches live context (kubectl, git, beads, personal notes) and produces a structured result\n27\t4. Results render as HTML cards on a live canvas, appearing as agents resolve\n28\t5. In audio mode, results are narrated at natural conversational pauses\n29\t\n30\tNo switching. No routing by hand. One surface for everything.\n31\t\n32\t```\n33\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n34\t│  Voice / Text Input                                  │\n35\t│  \"has options pipeline caught up? ibkr mcp status?\" │\n36\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n37\t                          │\n38\t                          ▼\n39\t               ┌──────────────────┐\n40\t               │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n41\t               │   (haiku-class)  │    tags each thread: project + intent_type\n42\t               └──────────────────┘\n43\t                          │\n44\t            ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n45\t            ▼                            ▼\n46\t     [agent: options-pipeline]   [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n47\t     Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n48\t            │                            │\n49\t            ▼                            ▼\n50\t      status card                  status card\n51\t                          │\n52\t                          ▼\n53\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n54\t│  Canvas                                             │\n55\t│  ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐         │\n56\t│  │ options-pipeline │   │ ibkr-mcp        │         │\n57\t│  │ [status card]   │   │ [status card]   │         │\n58\t│  └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘         │\n59\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n60\t```\n61\t\n62\t---\n63\t\n64\t## Key Properties\n65\t\n66\t### Universal scope\n67\tNot a coding assistant. Research, lookups, project status, personal context, reminders, brainstorming — any intent type the router can classify, any agent can handle.\n68\t\n69\t### Parallel dispatch\n70\tEach intent thread runs concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not the sum of all five.\n71\t\n72\t### Self-improving through conversation\n73\tAfter initial bootstrap, the interface improves itself by talking into it:\n74\t\n75\t- \"Always include pod restart count in status cards\" → synthesize prompt updated, hot-reloaded\n76\t- \"You always route 'the feed' to the wrong project\" → registry alias added\n77\t- \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" → UI-regen agent iterates the component\n78\t- \"Start monitoring the pipeline and tell me if it goes behind\" → monitoring rule created\n79\t\n80\tNo IDE switch. No PR. No deployment. The improvement loop collapses into the usage loop.\n81\t\n82\t### Hot-reload everywhere\n83\tAll behavior lives in data — prompt files, YAML registry, HTML component templates — not compiled code. Every artifact is read per-invocation. A change takes effect on the next utterance.\n84\t\n85\t### Two surfaces, one backend\n86\t- **Canvas (multimedia)**: HTML card grid, no voice output, results stream in as they resolve\n87\t- **Audio (mobile/Telegram)**: AI narrates results, no visual output, tool calls are triggers not queries\n88\t- Same result schema. Same session. Switch surfaces mid-session without losing state.\n89\t\n90\t### Human as exception handler\n91\tThe system handles everything automatically. Genuine blockers — decisions only the user can make — are routed to an exception queue and pushed to Telegram or the canvas exception thread. Everything else resolves without interruption.\n92\t\n93\t---\n94\t\n95\t## Architecture Overview\n96\t\n97\t### Hot path (< 3 seconds to first partial result)\n98\t\n99\t```\n100\tVoice/text input\n101\t  → STT (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n102\t  → Intent Router (one direct API call, haiku-class)\n103\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize strands (direct API calls, sonnet-class)\n104\t  → SSE stream to canvas\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\tNo Claude Code session startup on the critical path. All direct API calls via ZAI proxy.\n108\t\n109\t### Async path (task beads, not time-critical)\n110\t\n111\t```\n112\tTask intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n113\t  → NEEDLE task bead created\n114\t  → Claude Code worker picks up bead\n115\t  → Bead watcher detects closure\n116\t  → Result pushed to canvas via SSE or Telegram\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t### Self-improvement path (async, off critical path)\n120\t\n121\t```\n122\tFeedback (explicit instruction or implicit signal)\n123\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n124\t  → Reads artifact → generates update → shows diff\n125\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded\n126\t```\n127\t\n128\t### Agent runtimes\n129\t\n130\t| Runtime | Components |\n131\t|---------|-----------|\n132\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n133\t| Direct API via ZAI | Intent router, Synthesize strand, Escalate strand |\n134\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git/br execution), Bead watcher |\n135\t| Claude Code via NEEDLE | UI-regen agent, Self-modification agent, Background analysis, Task beads |\n136\t\n137\t---\n138\t\n139\t## Session and State Model\n140\t\n141\t**Session**: surface-agnostic persistent entity. The same session is accessible from the canvas, Telegram, or audio mode simultaneously. Switching surfaces does not reset context.\n142\t\n143\t**Topic**: a persistent concern (a project, a research thread, a recurring question). The canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place as new results arrive.\n144\t\n145\t**Result**: stored structured data (`{data, summary, urgency}`). Persists in the session store.\n146\t\n147\t**Card**: ephemeral rendering artifact. Generated from a result by the UI-regen agent using a component template. Not stored — regenerated as needed.\n148\t\n149\t**Component library**: versioned HTML/CSS templates generated by the UI-regen agent from actual usage. Starts empty, grows organically. Components are iterated when feedback (explicit or implicit) indicates they're serving poorly.\n150\t\n151\t---\n152\t\n153\t## Self-Improvement: What Can Be Changed Through Conversation\n154\t\n155\t| Category | Example instruction | Artifact updated |\n156\t|----------|--------------------|--------------------|\n157\t| Result format | \"Always include restart count in pod status\" | Synthesize strand prompt |\n158\t| Visual layout | \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" | Component iterated |\n159\t| Routing | \"'The feed' means options-aggregator\" | Project registry alias |\n160\t| Compound workflows | \"Add a workflow for deploying IBKR MCP\" | Registry workflow template |\n161\t| Monitoring | \"Watch the pipeline and alert if behind\" | Topic monitoring config |\n162\t| Voice behavior | \"Summaries are too long for audio\" | Voice model prompt |\n163\t| Urgency | \"Don't push pipeline updates unless critical\" | Urgency classifier prompt |\n164\t| New project | \"Add pdftract to the registry\" | Project registry |\n165\t| Exception handling | \"Auto-approve deploys to staging\" | Exception routing rules |\n166\t\n167\t---\n168\t\n169\t## CLI\n170\t\n171\t`adc` is the CLI invocation for aide-de-camp.\n172\t\n173\t```bash\n174\t# Dispatch a text utterance (bypasses web UI)\n175\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n176\t\n177\t# Query a specific topic\n178\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n179\t\n180\t# Check active session status\n181\tadc status\n182\t\n183\t# List active topics\n184\tadc topics\n185\t\n186\t# Show exception queue\n187\tadc exceptions\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\t---\n191\t\n192\t## Repository Structure\n193\t\n194\t```\n195\taide-de-camp/\n196\t├── README.md\n197\t├── docs/\n198\t│   ├── research/          ← architecture and design research\n199\t│   │   ├── overview.md          — system concept, components, open questions\n200\t│   │   ├── agent-runtimes.md    — which runtime powers each component\n201\t│   │   ├── component-library.md — cached, versioned, self-growing UI components\n202\t│   │   ├── context-model.md     — shallow registry + on-demand fetch\n203\t│   │   ├── cross-project-intents.md — project graph, workflow templates\n204\t│   │   ├── dispatch-architecture.md — queue-based dispatch, scaling path\n205\t│   │   ├── execution-model.md   — live vs. task profile, bead watcher\n206\t│   │   ├── interaction-modes.md — canvas vs. audio surfaces\n207\t│   │   ├── mayor-parallel.md    — parallel Mayor pattern, Gas City validation\n208\t│   │   ├── mayor-parallel-gascity-update.md — May 2026 Gas City state\n209\t│   │   ├── needle-strands.md    — corrected strand model, dispatch waterfall\n210\t│   │   ├── primitives.md        — what uses beads vs. session store\n211\t│   │   ├── resource-reuse.md    — existing infrastructure reused\n212\t│   │   ├── responsiveness.md    — latency, awareness, conversational quality\n213\t│   │   ├── self-improvement.md  — hot-reload, feedback loop, safety model\n214\t│   │   ├── session-model.md     — surface routing, DLQ exception model\n215\t│   │   └── topic-model.md       — topic/intent/result/card hierarchy\n216\t│   ├── plan/\n217\t│   │   └── roadmap.md           — phased implementation plan (Phase 0–4)\n218\t│   └── notes/\n219\t│       └── naming.md            — why aide-de-camp, why adc\n220\t```\n221\t\n222\t---\n223\t\n224\t## What Requires Code vs. Conversation\n225\t\n226\t### Requires a code change and deploy\n227\t- New context-fetch integrations (new APIs, new data sources)\n228\t- New tool implementations for NEEDLE workers\n229\t- Initial bootstrap (Phase 0 implementation)\n230\t\n231\t### Reachable through conversation after bootstrap\n232\t- All prompt tuning (router, synthesize, voice model, urgency classifier)\n233\t- Project registry (aliases, new projects, workflow templates)\n234\t- Component iteration (layout, fields, visual design)\n235\t- Monitoring rules and urgency thresholds\n236\t- Exception routing configuration\n237\t\n238\t---\n239\t\n240\t## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n241\t\n242\taide-de-camp is built on top of existing infrastructure, not alongside it:\n243\t\n244\t- **NEEDLE workers** — task beads unchanged; aide-de-camp adds a bead watcher to route closed-bead results to the canvas\n245\t- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused as always-available surface fallback and HUMAN bead push\n246\t- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; used as STT backend in audio mode\n247\t- **ZAI proxy** (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through this\n248\t- **claude-governor** — governs Claude Code subscription consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads\n249\t- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, and OpenAI Realtime API session management reused as the voice layer foundation\n250\t- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions; session conversational state lives in a separate SQLite session store\n251\t\n252\tNet-new code: approximately 1,400 lines of wiring across router, strand waterfall, bead watcher, session store, and canvas aide-de-camp\n\n**A universal personal interface. Speak or type stream-of-consciousness input across any domain — projects, research, lookups, personal context — and receive parallel, organized results on a live canvas or in audio.**\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## The Problem\n\nManaging multiple active projects means constant context-switching: open the right tab, re-establish context, ask the question, wait, switch to the next project, repeat. The input channel — typing into a specific session — is also the routing layer, and that conflation is the friction.\n\nBeyond projects: the same friction applies to research, personal notes, lookups, reminders, and anything else that requires knowing where to ask. You shouldn't have to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## What aide-de-camp Does\n\nYou speak or type without caring which project, domain, or system you're addressing:\n\n> \"Has the options pipeline caught up? Also what's the state of the IBKR MCP, and remind me what name candidates we had for pdftract.\"\n\naide-de-camp:\n1. Segments the utterance into intent threads and tags each with project and intent type\n2. Dispatches each thread to a parallel agent — one per thread, all running concurrently\n3. Each agent fetches live context (kubectl, git, beads, personal notes) and produces a structured result\n4. Results render as HTML cards on a live canvas, appearing as agents resolve\n5. In audio mode, results are narrated at natural conversational pauses\n\nNo switching. No routing by hand. One surface for everything.\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Voice / Text Input                                  │\n│  \"has options pipeline caught up? ibkr mcp status?\" │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                          │\n                          ▼\n               ┌──────────────────┐\n               │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n               │   (haiku-class)  │    tags each thread: project + intent_type\n               └──────────────────┘\n                          │\n            ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n            ▼                            ▼\n     [agent: options-pipeline]   [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n     Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n            │                            │\n            ▼                            ▼\n      status card                  status card\n                          │\n                          ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Canvas                                             │\n│  ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐         │\n│  │ options-pipeline │   │ ibkr-mcp        │         │\n│  │ [status card]   │   │ [status card]   │         │\n│  └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘         │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n## Key Properties\n\n### Universal scope\nNot a coding assistant. Research, lookups, project status, personal context, reminders, brainstorming — any intent type the router can classify, any agent can handle.\n\n### Parallel dispatch\nEach intent thread runs concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not the sum of all five.\n\n### Self-improving through conversation\nAfter initial bootstrap, the interface improves itself by talking into it:\n\n- \"Always include pod restart count in status cards\" → synthesize prompt updated, hot-reloaded\n- \"You always route 'the feed' to the wrong project\" → registry alias added\n- \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" → UI-regen agent iterates the component\n- \"Start monitoring the pipeline and tell me if it goes behind\" → monitoring rule created\n\nNo IDE switch. No PR. No deployment. The improvement loop collapses into the usage loop.\n\n### Hot-reload everywhere\nAll behavior lives in data — prompt files, YAML registry, HTML component templates — not compiled code. Every artifact is read per-invocation. A change takes effect on the next utterance.\n\n### Two surfaces, one backend\n- **Canvas (multimedia)**: HTML card grid, no voice output, results stream in as they resolve\n- **Audio (mobile/Telegram)**: AI narrates results, no visual output, tool calls are triggers not queries\n- Same result schema. Same session. Switch surfaces mid-session without losing state.\n\n### Human as exception handler\nThe system handles everything automatically. Genuine blockers — decisions only the user can make — are routed to an exception queue and pushed to Telegram or the canvas exception thread. Everything else resolves without interruption.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\n### Hot path (< 3 seconds to first partial result)\n\n```\nVoice/text input\n  → STT (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one direct API call, haiku-class)\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize strands (direct API calls, sonnet-class)\n  → SSE stream to canvas\n```\n\nNo Claude Code session startup on the critical path. All direct API calls via ZAI proxy.\n\n### Async path (task beads, not time-critical)\n\n```\nTask intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created\n  → Claude Code worker picks up bead\n  → Bead watcher detects closure\n  → Result pushed to canvas via SSE or Telegram\n```\n\n### Self-improvement path (async, off critical path)\n\n```\nFeedback (explicit instruction or implicit signal)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n  → Reads artifact → generates update → shows diff\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded\n```\n\n### Agent runtimes\n\n| Runtime | Components |\n|---------|-----------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Direct API via ZAI | Intent router, Synthesize strand, Escalate strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git/br execution), Bead watcher |\n| Claude Code via NEEDLE | UI-regen agent, Self-modification agent, Background analysis, Task beads |\n\n---\n\n## Session and State Model\n\n**Session**: surface-agnostic persistent entity. The same session is accessible from the canvas, Telegram, or audio mode simultaneously. Switching surfaces does not reset context.\n\n**Topic**: a persistent concern (a project, a research thread, a recurring question). The canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place as new results arrive.\n\n**Result**: stored structured data (`{data, summary, urgency}`). Persists in the session store.\n\n**Card**: ephemeral rendering artifact. Generated from a result by the UI-regen agent using a component template. Not stored — regenerated as needed.\n\n**Component library**: versioned HTML/CSS templates generated by the UI-regen agent from actual usage. Starts empty, grows organically. Components are iterated when feedback (explicit or implicit) indicates they're serving poorly.\n\n---\n\n## Self-Improvement: What Can Be Changed Through Conversation\n\n| Category | Example instruction | Artifact updated |\n|----------|--------------------|--------------------|\n| Result format | \"Always include restart count in pod status\" | Synthesize strand prompt |\n| Visual layout | \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" | Component iterated |\n| Routing | \"'The feed' means options-aggregator\" | Project registry alias |\n| Compound workflows | \"Add a workflow for deploying IBKR MCP\" | Registry workflow template |\n| Monitoring | \"Watch the pipeline and alert if behind\" | Topic monitoring config |\n| Voice behavior | \"Summaries are too long for audio\" | Voice model prompt |\n| Urgency | \"Don't push pipeline updates unless critical\" | Urgency classifier prompt |\n| New project | \"Add pdftract to the registry\" | Project registry |\n| Exception handling | \"Auto-approve deploys to staging\" | Exception routing rules |\n\n---\n\n## CLI\n\n`adc` is the CLI invocation for aide-de-camp.\n\n```bash\n# Dispatch a text utterance (bypasses web UI)\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Check active session status\nadc status\n\n# List active topics\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n```\n\n---\n\n## Repository Structure\n\n```\naide-de-camp/\n├── README.md\n├── docs/\n│   ├── research/          ← architecture and design research\n│   │   ├── overview.md          — system concept, components, open questions\n│   │   ├── agent-runtimes.md    — which runtime powers each component\n│   │   ├── component-library.md — cached, versioned, self-growing UI components\n│   │   ├── context-model.md     — shallow registry + on-demand fetch\n│   │   ├── cross-project-intents.md — project graph, workflow templates\n│   │   ├── dispatch-architecture.md — queue-based dispatch, scaling path\n│   │   ├── execution-model.md   — live vs. task profile, bead watcher\n│   │   ├── interaction-modes.md — canvas vs. audio surfaces\n│   │   ├── mayor-parallel.md    — parallel Mayor pattern, Gas City validation\n│   │   ├── mayor-parallel-gascity-update.md — May 2026 Gas City state\n│   │   ├── needle-strands.md    — corrected strand model, dispatch waterfall\n│   │   ├── primitives.md        — what uses beads vs. session store\n│   │   ├── resource-reuse.md    — existing infrastructure reused\n│   │   ├── responsiveness.md    — latency, awareness, conversational quality\n│   │   ├── self-improvement.md  — hot-reload, feedback loop, safety model\n│   │   ├── session-model.md     — surface routing, DLQ exception model\n│   │   └── topic-model.md       — topic/intent/result/card hierarchy\n│   ├── plan/\n│   │   └── roadmap.md           — phased implementation plan (Phase 0–4)\n│   └── notes/\n│       └── naming.md            — why aide-de-camp, why adc\n```\n\n---\n\n## What Requires Code vs. Conversation\n\n### Requires a code change and deploy\n- New context-fetch integrations (new APIs, new data sources)\n- New tool implementations for NEEDLE workers\n- Initial bootstrap (Phase 0 implementation)\n\n### Reachable through conversation after bootstrap\n- All prompt tuning (router, synthesize, voice model, urgency classifier)\n- Project registry (aliases, new projects, workflow templates)\n- Component iteration (layout, fields, visual design)\n- Monitoring rules and urgency thresholds\n- Exception routing configuration\n\n---\n\n## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n\naide-de-camp is built on top of existing infrastructure, not alongside it:\n\n- **NEEDLE workers** — task beads unchanged; aide-de-camp adds a bead watcher to route closed-bead results to the canvas\n- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused as always-available surface fallback and HUMAN bead push\n- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; used as STT backend in audio mode\n- **ZAI proxy** (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through this\n- **claude-governor** — governs Claude Code subscription consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads\n- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, and OpenAI Realtime API session management reused as the voice layer foundation\n- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions; session conversational state lives in a separate SQLite session store\n\nNet-new code: approximately 1,400 lines of wiring across router, strand waterfall, bead watcher, session store, and canvas frontend.\n","numLines":253,"startLine":1,"totalLines":253}}}

}\n414\t\n415\t        function formatStaleness(seconds) {\n416\t            if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n417\t            if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n418\t            if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n419\t            return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n420\t        }\n421\t\n422\t        function 'very-stale';\n426\t        }\n427\t\n428\t        function createTopicCard(cardData) {\n429\t            const topic = cardData.topic;\n430\t            const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n431\t `;\n448\t\n449\t            // Add latest result if available\n450\t            if (latestResult) {\n451\t                const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n452\t `;\n459\t\n460\t                if (latestResult.data) {\n461\t                    html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n462\t ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n472\t                    <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n473\t                </div>\n474\t            `;\n475\t\n476\t            card.innerHTML = html;\n477\t            return card;\n478\t        }\n479\t\n480\t        function escapeHtml(text) {\n481\t            const div = document.createElement('div');\n482\t            div.textContent = text;\n483\t            return div.innerHTML;\n484\t        }\n485\t\n486\t        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n487\t            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n488\t            const   function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n495\t            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n496\t            const parts = [];\n497\t\n498\t            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n499\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n500\t            }\n501\t            if (summary.new_results > 0)   }\n515\t\n516\t        async function registerSurface() {\n517\t            try {\n518\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n519\t                    method: {\n709\t                console.error('Speech recognition error:', e.error);\n710\t                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n711\t            };\n712\t\n713\t Cancel listener task\n326\t        if listener_task:\n327\t            listener_task.cancel()\n328\t            try:\n329\t                await listener_task\n330\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n331\t                pass\n332\t\n333\t        # Mark surface disconnected\n334\t        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n335\t        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n336\t\n337\t\n338\t@app.post(\"/router\")\n339\tasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n340\t    \"\"\"\n341\t    Intent router endpoint.\n342\t\n343\t    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n344\t    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n345\t    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n346\t    \"\"\"\n347\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n348\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n349\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n350\t\n351\t    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n352\t\n353\t    store = await get_store()\n354\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n355\t\n356\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n357\t    if not utterance_id:\n358\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n359\t\n360\t    # Create utterance record\n361\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n362\t\n363\t    # Route the utterance\n364\t    try:\n365\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n366\t            utterance=utterance,\n367\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n368\t            session_id=session_id,\n369\t        )\n370\t\n371\t        # Process each routed intent\n372\t        intent_results = []\n373\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n374\t            # Create intent record in store\n375\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n376\t            await store.create_intent(\n377\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n378\t                session_id=session_id,\n379\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n380\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n381\t            )\n382\t\n383\t            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n384\t            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n385\t            intent_results.append(result)\n386\t\n387\t        return {\n388\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n389\t            \"intents\": intent_results,\n390\t        }\n391\t\n392\t    except Exception as e:\n393\t        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n394\t        return JSONResponse(\n395\t            status_code=500,\n396\t            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n397\t        )\n398\t\n399\t\n400\t@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\n401\tasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n402\t    \"\"\"\n403\t    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n404\t\n405\t    Phase 0 core query loop:\n406\t    1. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n410\t\n411\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n412\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n413\t    \"\"\"\n414\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n415\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n416\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n417\t    surface_id = get_intent_router(store)\n423\t\n424\t    # Ensure session exists (SSE endpoint may have created a different one)\n425\t    if session_id:\n426\t        existing = await store.create_session()\n431\t\n432\t    # Create utterance record (store generates and returns the ID)\n433\t    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance)\n434\t\n435\t    # Route the utterance\n436\t    try:\n437\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n438\t            utterance=utterance,\n439\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n440\t            session_id=session_id,\n441\t        )\n442\t\n443\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n444\t        intent_tasks = []\n445\t        intent_ids = []\n446\t\n447\t        for              utterance_id=utterance_id,\n452\t                session_id=session_id,\n453\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n454\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n455\t            )\n456\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n457\t\n458\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n459\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n460\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n461\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n462\t            )\n463\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n464\t\n465\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n466\t        async def stream_results():\n467\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n468\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n469\t                try:\n470\t                    result = await task\n471\t                    if result.get(\"status\") == \"resolved\":\n472\t                        project_slug = result.get(\"project_slug\") or \"general\"\n473\t                        topic_id, _ = await project_slugs=[project_slug],\n477\t                            session_id=session_id,\n478\t                        )\n479\t                        result_id = await store.create_result(\n480\t                            intent_id=intent_id,\n481\t                            topic_id=topic_id,\n482\t                            session_id=session_id,\n483\t                            summary=result.get(\"summary\", \"\"),\n484\t                            data=result.get(\"data\", {}),\n485\t                            urgency=result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n486\t                        )\n487\t                        if _broadcaster:\n488\t                            await _broadcaster.broadcast(SSEEvent(\n489\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n490\t                                target_session_id=session_id,\n491\t                                data={\n492\t                                    \"result_id\": \"normal\"),\n498\t                                }\n499\t                            ))\n500\t                except Exception as e:\n501\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n502\t\n503\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n504\t\n505\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n506\t        return {\n507\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n508\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n509\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n510\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n511\t logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n517\t        return JSONResponse(\n518\t            status_code=500,\n519\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n520\t        )\n521\t\n522\t\n523\t@app.post(\"/escalate\")\n524\tasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n525\t    \"\"\"\n526\t    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async serve_canvas():\n    \"\"\"\n    Serve the canvas HTML page (Phase 0 minimal surface).\n\n    This is the main entry point for the aide-de-camp interface.\n    \"\"\"\n    return FileResponse(CANVAS_PATH)\n\n\n@app.websocket(\"/voice\")\nasync def voice_session(websocket: WebSocket):\n    \"\"\"\n    Voice session endpoint using OpenAI Realtime API.\n\n    Handles:\n    - WebRTC connection establishment\n    - Tool-as-trigger dispatch_intent calls\n    - Async result delivery for narration\n    - Surface switch events (audio-to-canvas continuity)\n    \"\"\"\n    await websocket.accept()\n\n    # Get session ID from query param or create new\n    session_id = websocket.query_params.get(\"session_id\") or str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    logger.info(f\"Voice session connecting: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Get API key from environment\n    api_key = os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\")\n    if not api_key:\n        await websocket.send_json({\n            \"type\": \"error\",\n            \"error\": \"OpenAI API key not configured\"\n        })\n        await websocket.close(code=1011, reason=\"API key missing\")\n        return\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"audio\")\n    surface_id = audio_surface_id\n    logger.info(f\"Registered audio surface: {surface_id}\")\n\n    # Load voice prompt\n    voice_prompt = load_voice_prompt(VOICE_PROMPT_PATH)\n\n    # Surface switch handler for audio-to-canvas continuity\n    async def on_surface_switch(surface_type: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle surface switch event (audio -> canvas).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Surface switch: {surface_type}\")\n        try:\n            result = await handle_surface_switch(surface_type, session_id, voice)\n            # Send acknowledgment to client\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n                \"surface\": surface_type,\n                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=None,  # TODO: add memory extraction\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n        )\n\n        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n            description=(\n                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Run the session\n        await voice.run()\n\n    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e)\n            })\n        except:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Mark surface disconnected\n        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n\n\n@app.post(\"/router\")\nasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent router endpoint.\n\n    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Ensure session exists (SSE endpoint may have created a different one)\n    if session_id:\n        existing = await store.get_session(session_id)\n        if not existing:\n            await store.create_session(session_id)\n    else:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n\n    # Create utterance record (store generates and returns the ID)\n    utterance_id = await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    if result.get(\"status\") == \"resolved\":\n                        project_slug = result.get(\"project_slug\") or \"general\"\n                        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                            label=project_slug,\n                            topic_type=\"project\",\n                            project_slugs=[project_slug],\n                            session_id=session_id,\n                        )\n                        result_id = await store.create_result(\n                            intent_id=intent_id,\n                            topic_id=topic_id,\n                            session_id=session_id,\n                            summary=result.get(\"summary\", \"\"),\n                            data=result.get(\"data\", {}),\n                            urgency=result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                        )\n                        if _broadcaster:\n                            await _broadcaster.broadcast(SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_session_id=session_id,\n                                data={\n                                    \"result_id\": result_id,\n                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                                    \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\", \"\"),\n                                    \"data\": result.get(\"data\", {}),\n                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                                }\n                            ))\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via br CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        utterance=utterance,\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        project_slug=project_slug,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n        context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = intent_id\n\n    try:\n        # Escalate to bead\n        result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {escalate_request.intent_id} to bead {result.bead_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": result.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n            \"status\": result.status,\n            \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session (preserve provided session_id)\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session(session_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session (preserve provided session_id)\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def api_v1_heartbeat(request: HeartbeatRequest):\n    \"\"\"Update surface heartbeat.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n    await           yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",\n        },\n    )\n\n\n# Feedback and Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve and apply a pending change.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.approve_change(request.approval_id)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/reject\")\nasync def api_v1_reject_change(request: content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.reject_change(request.approval_id, reason)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    return {\n        \"pending\": _feedback_processor.list_pending_approvals()\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/rollback\")\nasync def api_v1_rollback(request: RollbackRequest):\n    \"\"\"Rollback an artifact to its previous version.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    artifact_type_map = {\n        \"prompt\": ArtifactType.PROMPT,\n        \"config\": ArtifactType.CONFIG,\n        \"component\": ArtifactType.COMPONENT,\n    }\n\n    artifact_type = artifact_type_map.get(\n        request.artifact_type,\n        ArtifactType.PROMPT\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.rollback(\n lines.append(f\"```\")\n                else:\n                    lines.append(f\"Status: {source_result.status}\")\n                    if source_result.error:\n                        lines.append(f\"Error: {source_result.error}\")\n\n            # Add coverage info\n            if request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                lines.append(f\"\\n## Caveats\")\n                for caveat in request.fetched_context.caveats:\n                    lines.append(f\"- {caveat}\")\n\n        lines.append(f\"\\nPlease synthesize this into a structured result.\")\n        return
