store implementation using SQLite with WAL mode.\n\nStores sessions, surfaces, utterances, intents, results, topics, and intent_topics.\nProvides concurrent read access with serialized writes via WAL mode.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport aiosqlite\nimport sqlite3\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\nimport httpx\n\n# Schema definition\nSCHEMA_SQL = \"\"\"\n-- Sessions: surface-agnostic persistent entities\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    primary_surface_id TEXT\n);\n\n-- Surfaces: transient windows into sessions\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS surfaces (\n    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('canvas', 'telegram', 'audio')),\n    state           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(state IN ('active', 'idle', 'disconnected')) DEFAULT 'active',\n    always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(always_available IN (0, 1)),\n    last_seen       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_session ON surfaces(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_surfaces_state ON surfaces(state);\n\n-- Utterances: raw input from user\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS utterances (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    raw_text    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_utterances_session ON utterances(session_id);\n\n-- Intents: parsed intent threads from utterances\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intents (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    project_slug TEXT,\n    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n    bead_ref     TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_session ON intents(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_topic ON intents(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_intents_status ON intents(status);\n\n-- Results: structured data returned by agents\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS results (\n    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    intent_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n    data        TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON\n    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n    acked_at    INTEGER,\n    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n\n-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n    session_id   TEXT,\n    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    archived_at  INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_session ON topics(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_scope ON topics(scope);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_active ON topics(last_active DESC);\n\n-- Topic context cache: pre-warmed context for active topics\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topic_context_cache (\n    topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n\n-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\nCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n    result_id    TEXT,\n    topic_id     TEXT,\n    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n    surface_type TEXT,\n    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n    processed_at INTEGER,\n    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n\"\"\"\n\n\nclass SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n        self.db_path = db_path\n\n    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n\n    # Session operations\n    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        if not session_id:\n            session_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                (session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return session_id\n\n    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Surface operations\n    async def register_surface(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n        always_available: bool = False,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        surface_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO surfaces\n                   (id, session_id, type, state, always_available, last_seen)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'active', ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (surface_id, session_id, surface_type, 1 if always_available else 0, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return surface_id\n\n    async def update_surface_heartbeat(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update surface last_seen timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET last_seen = ?, state = 'active' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, surface_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_surface_disconnected(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a surface as disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET state = 'disconnected' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_surfaces(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all active surfaces for a session, ordered by last_seen.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND state IN ('active', 'idle')\n                   ORDER BY last_seen DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_fallback_surface(self) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the always-available fallback surface (Telegram).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE always_available = 1 AND state != 'disconnected'\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Utterance operations\n    async def create_utterance(self, session_id: str, raw_text: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new utterance and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        utterance_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO utterances (id, session_id, raw_text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n                (utterance_id, session_id, raw_text, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return utterance_id\n\n    # Intent operations\n    async def create_intent(\n        self,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new intent and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        intent_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO intents\n                   (id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, bead_ref, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (intent_id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, bead_ref, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return intent_id\n\n    async def update_intent_status(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        status: str,\n        resolved_at: int | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update intent status.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            if resolved_at is None and status == \"resolved\":\n                resolved_at = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE intents SET status = ?, resolved_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (status, resolved_at, intent_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_pending_intents(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending intents for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    # Result operations\n    async def create_result(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        previous_result_id: str | None = None,\n        diff_summary: str | None = None,\n        diff_data: dict | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new result and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        result_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO results\n                   (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, created_at, surfaced_at,\n                    previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    result_id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, json.dumps(data),\n                    urgency, now, now, previous_result_id, diff_summary,\n                    json.dumps(diff_data) if diff_data else None\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return result_id\n\n    async def get_unsurfed_results(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get results that haven't been surfaced to the user yet.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at ASC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark all unsurfed results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed_by_ids(self, session_id: str, result_ids: list[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark specific results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for result_id in result_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND session_id = ?\",\n                    (now, result_id, session_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get active topics for a session, ordered by last_active.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT t.*,\n                          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results r WHERE r.topic_id = t.id) as result_count\n                   FROM topics t\n                   WHERE (t.session_id = ? OR t.scope IN ('cross-session', 'global'))\n                     AND t.archived_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY t.last_active DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_latest_result_for_topic(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the most recent result for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE topic_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def link_intent_to_topic(self, intent_id: str, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Link an intent to a topic (many-to-many).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO intent_topics (intent_id, topic_id) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Workload summary for reconnect\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get summary of what happened while surface was disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Get pending intents\n            pending_count = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intents WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (surfaced after last surface disconnect)\n            # For now, return count of results in last hour\n            one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND created_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, one_hour_ago)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (one_hour_ago,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n\n    # Feedback signal operations\n    async def create_feedback_signal(\n        self,\n        signal_id: str,\n        signal_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        result_id: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n        timestamp: int | None = None,\n        data: dict | None = None,\n        surface_type: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO feedback_signals\n                   (signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id,\n                    topic_id, timestamp or now, json.dumps(data or {}),\n                    surface_type\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return signal_id\n\n    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n            params = []\n\n            if signal_type:\n                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n                params.append(signal_type)\n\n            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n            params.append(limit)\n\n            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_signals_processed(\n        self,\n        signal_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE signal_id = ?\",\n                    (now, signal_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n    async def create_result_with_diff(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n\n        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n\n        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n\n        # Get previous result for this topic\n        previous_result = await self.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n\n        # Compute diff if we have a previous result\n        diff_summary = None\n        diff_data = None\n        has_diff = False\n\n        if previous_result:\n            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n            diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                previous_result=previous_result,\n                current_result={\"data\": data},\n            )\n\n            if diff_result.has_changes:\n                has_diff = True\n                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n                diff_data = {\n                    \"fields\": [\n                        {\n                            \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                            \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                            \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                            \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                        }\n                        for f in diff_result.fields\n                    ],\n                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n                }\n\n        # Create the result with diff data\n        result_id = await self.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency,\n            previous_result_id=previous_result[\"id\"] if previous_result else None,\n            diff_summary=diff_summary,\n            diff_data=diff_data,\n        )\n\n        return result_id, has_diff\n\n\n# Global session store instance\n_store: SessionStore | None = None\n\n\ndef get_store(db_path: Path | None = None) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global session store instance.\"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        if db_path is None:\n            db_path = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/session.db\")\n        _store = SessionStore(db_path)\n    return _store\n","numLines":805,"startLine":1,"totalLines":805}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"file_path\":\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/notes/adc-56i.md\",\"content\":\"# Text Representation Truncation - Implementation Plan\\n\\n## Executive Summary\\n\\nThis document outlines the implementation requirements for adding text truncation to the aide-de-camp application. The feature addresses the need to limit display length for long text representations (summaries and data) on the canvas while preserving full content for other uses (audio mode, data export).\\n\\n## Current State Analysis\\n\\n### Where Text is Stored\\n**Database**: `data/session.db` → `results` table\\n- `summary` (TEXT): Conversational summary generated by LLM\\n- `data` (TEXT/JSON): Structured result data\\n\\n### Where Text is Rendered\\n**File**: `src/canvas/index.html`\\n- `.result-summary` (line 452-456): Displays summary text\\n- `.result-data` (line 460-462): Displays preformatted JSON data\\n\\n### Where Text is Generated\\n**File**: `src/synthesize/strand.py`\\n- `SynthesizeResult.summary` (line 53): Generated by LLM call\\n- `SynthesizeResult.data` (line 52): Structured data from fetch sources\\n\\n**File**: `prompts/synthesize.md`\\n- Instructs LLM to generate \\\"2-3 sentence narration\\\" (line 30) — **no hard character limit enforced**\\n\\n### Existing Constraints\\n- `max_height: 150px` with `overflow-y: auto` for result data (CSS, line 201) — **scroll-based, not truncation**\\n- No character limits on summary text\\n- JSON data can be arbitrarily large\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Implementation Plan\\n\\n### Option A: Application Layer Truncation (Recommended)\\n\\n**Rationale**: Application-layer truncation allows for:\\n- Smart truncation at word/sentence boundaries\\n- Context-aware \\\"...\\\" indicators\\n- Full-text preservation in database\\n- Different truncation rules per surface type (canvas vs audio)\\n\\n#### 1. Add Truncation Utility Module\\n\\n**File**: `src/utils/text.py` (new file)\\n\\n```python\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nText processing utilities for aide-de-camp.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nimport unicodedata\\nfrom typing import Optional\\n\\n\\n# Default truncation limits (configurable)\\nSUMMARY_MAX_CHARS = 280  # ~2-3 sentences\\nSUMMARY_MAX_SENTENCES = 3\\nDATA_JSON_MAX_CHARS = 1000  # For display purposes\\n\\n\\ndef truncate_at_boundary(\\n    text: str,\\n    max_chars: int,\\n    respect_sentence: bool = True,\\n    ellipsis: str = \\\"...\\\"\\n) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Truncate text at a natural boundary (word or sentence).\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        text: Input text to truncate\\n        max_chars: Maximum character limit\\n        respect_sentence: If True, truncate at sentence boundary when possible\\n        ellipsis: String to append when truncated\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Truncated text with ellipsis if shortened\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    if not text or len(text) <= max_chars:\\n        return text\\n    \\n    # Reserve space for ellipsis\\n    effective_max = max_chars - len(ellipsis)\\n    \\n    if respect_sentence:\\n        # Try to truncate at sentence boundary\\n        truncated = text[:effective_max]\\n        last_period = truncated.rfind('.')\\n        if last_period > effective_max // 2:  # At least halfway through\\n            return text[:last_period + 1] + ellipsis\\n    \\n    # Fallback to word boundary\\n    words = text[:effective_max].split()\\n    if len(words) > 1:\\n        return ' '.join(words[:-1]) + ellipsis\\n    \\n    # Last resort: hard truncate\\n    return text[:effective_max] + ellipsis\\n\\n\\ndef truncate_sentences(text: str, max_sentences: int, ellipsis: str = \\\"...\\\") -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Truncate text to a maximum number of sentences.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        text: Input text to truncate\\n        max_sentences: Maximum number of sentences to keep\\n        ellipsis: String to append when truncated\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Text truncated to sentence limit\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    if not text:\\n        return text\\n    \\n    # Split by sentence boundaries (. ! ?)\\n    sentences = []\\n    current = []\\n    for char in text:\\n        current.append(char)\\n        if char in '.!?':\\n            sentence = ''.join(current).strip()\\n            if sentence:\\n                sentences.append(sentence)\\n            current = []\\n    \\n    # Add any remaining text as partial sentence\\n    if current:\\n        remaining = ''.join(current).strip()\\n        if remaining and len(sentences) < max_sentences:\\n            sentences.append(remaining)\\n    \\n    if len(sentences) <= max_sentences:\\n        return text\\n    \\n    return ' '.join(sentences[:max_sentences]) + ellipsis\\n\\n\\ndef truncate_json_display(json_str: str, max_chars: int = DATA_JSON_MAX_CHARS) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Truncate JSON string for display purposes.\\n    \\n    Preserves JSON structure by truncating at closing braces/brackets.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        json_str: JSON string to truncate\\n        max_chars: Maximum character limit for display\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Truncated JSON string with placeholder for omitted content\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    if not json_str or len(json_str) <= max_chars:\\n        return json_str\\n    \\n    # Simple heuristic: truncate at a closing bracket\\n    truncated = json_str[:max_chars]\\n    \\n    # Find last closing bracket/brace\\n    for i in range(len(truncated) - 1, -1, -1):\\n        if truncated[i] in '}]':\\n            return truncated[:i + 1] + '\\\\n  ... (truncated)'\\n    \\n    # Last resort: hard truncate with marker\\n    return truncated + '...'\\n\\n\\ndef count_grapheme_clusters(text: str) -> int:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Count user-perceived characters (grapheme clusters).\\n    \\n    Handles emoji, combining characters, and other Unicode complexities.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        text: Input text\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Count of grapheme clusters\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    # Simple implementation using unicodedata normalization\\n    # For full grapheme support, consider `grapheme` library\\n    normalized = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', text)\\n    count = 0\\n    prev_category = None\\n    \\n    for char in normalized:\\n        category = unicodedata.category(char)\\n        # Skip combining marks (categories Mn, Mc, Me)\\n        if category.startswith('M'):\\n            continue\\n        count += 1\\n    \\n    return count\\n\\n\\ndef safe_truncate(text: str, max_chars: int) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Truncate text safely, handling Unicode edge cases.\\n    \\n    Args:\\n        text: Input text (may contain emoji, combining characters)\\n        max_chars: Maximum grapheme clusters to display\\n    \\n    Returns:\\n        Truncated text with ellipsis if needed\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    if not text:\\n        return text\\n    \\n    # Count graphemes instead of code points\\n    cluster_count = count_grapheme_clusters(text)\\n    \\n    if cluster_count <= max_chars:\\n        return text\\n    \\n    # Use boundary-aware truncation\\n    return truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars * 2, respect_sentence=False)\\n```\\n\\n#### 2. Update Canvas HTML to Use Truncation\\n\\n**File**: `src/canvas/index.html`\\n\\n**Changes needed**:\\n1. Add JavaScript truncation helper functions\\n2. Apply truncation when rendering topic cards\\n\\n**Insert after `escapeHtml` function (around line 484)**:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// Text truncation helpers\\nconst TRUNCATION_LIMITS = {\\n    summary_chars: 280,\\n    data_chars: 1000,\\n};\\n\\nfunction truncateText(text, maxChars, respectSentence = true) {\\n    if (!text || text.length <= maxChars) return text;\\n    \\n    const effectiveMax = maxChars - 3; // Reserve space for \\\"...\\\"\\n    \\n    if (respectSentence) {\\n        // Try to truncate at sentence boundary\\n        const truncated = text.substring(0, effectiveMax);\\n        const lastPeriod = truncated.lastIndexOf('.');\\n        if (lastPeriod > effectiveMax / 2) {\\n            return text.substring(0, lastPeriod + 1) + '...';\\n        }\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Fallback to word boundary\\n    const words = text.substring(0, effectiveMax).split(/\\\\s+/);\\n    if (words.length > 1) {\\n        return words.slice(0, -1).join(' ') + '...';\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Hard truncate\\n    return text.substring(0, effectiveMax) + '...';\\n}\\n\\nfunction truncateJsonDisplay(jsonStr, maxChars = TRUNCATION_LIMITS.data_chars) {\\n    if (!jsonStr || jsonStr.length <= maxChars) return jsonStr;\\n    \\n    // Try to truncate at a closing bracket\\n    const truncated = jsonStr.substring(0, maxChars);\\n    for (let i = truncated.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\\n        if (truncated[i] === '}' || truncated[i] === ']') {\\n            return truncated.substring(0, i + 1) + '\\\\n  ... (truncated)';\\n        }\\n    }\\n    \\n    return truncated + '...';\\n}\\n\\nfunction escapeAndTruncate(text, maxChars, respectSentence = true) {\\n    return truncateText(escapeHtml(text), maxChars, respectSentence);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Modify `createTopicCard` function (around line 428)**:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// Find and replace the summary rendering (around line 454)\\n// OLD:\\n// <div class=\\\"result-summary\\\">\\n//     ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\\n//     <span class=\\\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\\\">${urgencyClass}</span>\\n// </div>\\n\\n// NEW:\\n<div class=\\\"result-summary\\\">\\n    ${escapeAndTruncate(latestResult.summary, TRUNCATION_LIMITS.summary_chars)}\\n    <span class=\\\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\\\">${urgencyClass}</span>\\n</div>\\n\\n// Find and replace the data rendering (around line 460-462)\\n// OLD:\\n// <div class=\\\"result-data\\\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>\\n\\n// NEW:\\n<div class=\\\"result-data\\\">${truncateJsonDisplay(escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2)))}</div>\\n```\\n\\n#### 3. Add Configuration Support\\n\\n**File**: `src/config.py` (new file)\\n\\n```python\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nConfiguration settings for aide-de-camp.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n\\n@dataclass\\nclass TruncationConfig:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Text truncation configuration.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    # Summary truncation\\n    summary_max_chars: int = 280\\n    summary_max_sentences: int = 3\\n    summary_truncate_at_sentence: bool = True\\n    \\n    # Data display truncation\\n    data_json_max_chars: int = 1000\\n    \\n    # Whether to respect grapheme clusters (emoji-aware)\\n    unicode_aware: bool = True\\n\\n\\n# Default configuration\\nDEFAULT_TRUNCATION = TruncationConfig()\\n\\n\\ndef get_truncation_config() -> TruncationConfig:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the current truncation configuration.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return DEFAULT_TRUNCATION\\n\\n\\ndef load_truncation_config_from_file(config_path: Path) -> TruncationConfig:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Load truncation configuration from a YAML file.\\n    \\n    This allows runtime reconfiguration without code changes.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    import yaml\\n    \\n    with open(config_path) as f:\\n        config_data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}\\n    \\n    truncation_data = config_data.get('truncation', {})\\n    return TruncationConfig(\\n        summary_max_chars=truncation_data.get('summary_max_chars', 280),\\n        summary_max_sentences=truncation_data.get('summary_max_sentences', 3),\\n        summary_truncate_at_sentence=truncation_data.get('summary_truncate_at_sentence', True),\\n        data_json_max_chars=truncation_data.get('data_json_max_chars', 1000),\\n        unicode_aware=truncation_data.get('unicode_aware', True),\\n    )\\n```\\n\\n**File**: `config/truncation.yaml` (new file)\\n\\n```yaml\\n# Text Truncation Configuration\\n\\ntruncation:\\n  # Summary text limits\\n  summary_max_chars: 280      # ~2-3 sentences\\n  summary_max_sentences: 3    # Maximum sentences before truncation\\n  summary_truncate_at_sentence: true  # Prefer sentence boundary\\n\\n  # Data display limits\\n  data_json_max_chars: 1000   # Characters before JSON truncation\\n\\n  # Unicode handling\\n  unicode_aware: true         # Count grapheme clusters (emoji-aware)\\n```\\n\\n#### 4. Update Topic Card Response\\n\\n**File**: `src/topic/model.py`\\n\\n**Add a method to `TopicCard` class**:\\n\\n```python\\n@dataclass\\nclass TopicCard:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"A topic card for the canvas - includes staleness info.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    topic: Topic\\n    latest_result: Optional[dict] = None\\n    staleness_seconds: int = 0\\n    staleness_level: str = \\\"fresh\\\"\\n    \\n    def to_dict(self, use_truncation: bool = True) -> dict:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Convert to dictionary, optionally truncating text fields.\\n        \\n        Args:\\n            use_truncation: If True, apply truncation to summary and data\\n        \\n        Returns:\\n            Dictionary representation of the topic card\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        from ..utils.text import truncate_at_boundary, truncate_json_display\\n        \\n        result = {\\n            \\\"topic\\\": self.topic.to_dict(),\\n            \\\"staleness\\\": {\\n                \\\"seconds\\\": self.staleness_seconds,\\n                \\\"level\\\": self.staleness_level,\\n            },\\n        }\\n        \\n        if self.latest_result:\\n            latest = dict(self.latest_result)\\n            \\n            if use_truncation:\\n                # Apply truncation to summary\\n                if \\\"summary\\\" in latest:\\n                    latest[\\\"summary\\\"] = truncate_at_boundary(\\n                        latest[\\\"summary\\\"],\\n                        max_chars=280,\\n                        respect_sentence=True\\n                    )\\n                \\n                # Apply truncation to data JSON representation\\n                if \\\"data\\\" in latest:\\n                    import json\\n                    data_json = json.dumps(latest[\\\"data\\\"], indent=2)\\n                    latest[\\\"data_truncated\\\"] = truncate_json_display(data_json, max_chars=1000)\\n            \\n            result[\\\"latest_result\\\"] = latest\\n        \\n        return result\\n```\\n\\n#### 5. Testing Strategy\\n\\n**File**: `tests/test_text_truncation.py` (new file)\\n\\n```python\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nTests for text truncation utilities.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nimport pytest\\nfrom src.utils.text import (\\n    truncate_at_boundary,\\n    truncate_sentences,\\n    truncate_json_display,\\n    count_grapheme_clusters,\\n    safe_truncate,\\n)\\n\\n\\nclass TestTruncateAtBoundary:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Tests for truncate_at_boundary function.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_no_truncation_short_text(self):\\n        text = \\\"Short text.\\\"\\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=100)\\n        assert result == text\\n    \\n    def test_truncation_with_ellipsis(self):\\n        text = \\\"This is a very long text that should be truncated.\\\"\\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=20)\\n        assert \\\"...\\\" in result\\n        assert len(result) <= 23  # 20 + \\\"...\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_respects_sentence_boundary(self):\\n        text = \\\"First sentence. Second sentence. Third sentence.\\\"\\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=30, respect_sentence=True)\\n        # Should truncate after \\\"First sentence.\\\"\\n        assert \\\"First sentence.\\\" in result\\n        assert result.endswith(\\\"...\\\")\\n    \\n    def test_word_boundary_fallback(self):\\n        text = \\\"Word1 Word2 Word3 Word4\\\"\\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=15, respect_sentence=False)\\n        # Should truncate at word boundary\\n        assert \\\"Word1\\\" in result\\n        assert result.endswith(\\\"...\\\")\\n\\n\\nclass TestTruncateSentences:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Tests for truncate_sentences function.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_no_truncation_fewer_sentences(self):\\n        text = \\\"First. Second.\\\"\\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=3)\\n        assert result == text\\n    \\n    def test_truncates_at_sentence_limit(self):\\n        text = \\\"First. Second. Third. Fourth.\\\"\\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=2)\\n        assert \\\"First.\\\" in result\\n        assert \\\"Second.\\\" in result\\n        assert \\\"Third.\\\" not in result\\n        assert result.endswith(\\\"...\\\")\\n    \\n    def test_handles_exclamation(self):\\n        text = \\\"First! Second! Third!\\\"\\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=2)\\n        assert result.count(\\\"!\\\") <= 2\\n    \\n    def test_handles_question(self):\\n        text = \\\"First? Second? Third?\\\"\\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=2)\\n        assert result.count(\\\"?\\\") <= 2\\n\\n\\nclass TestTruncateJsonDisplay:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Tests for JSON display truncation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_no_truncation_short_json(self):\\n        json_str = '{\\\"key\\\": \\\"value\\\"}'\\n        result = truncate_json_display(json_str, max_chars=100)\\n        assert result == json_str\\n    \\n    def test_truncates_at_closing_brace(self):\\n        json_str = '{\\\"items\\\": [{\\\"a\\\": 1}, {\\\"b\\\": 2}, {\\\"c\\\": 3}, {\\\"d\\\": 4}]}'\\n        result = truncate_json_display(json_str, max_chars=30)\\n        assert \\\"...\\\" in result or \\\"(truncated)\\\" in result\\n        assert result.startswith(\\\"{\\\")\\n    \\n    def test_preserves_structure(self):\\n        json_str = '{\\\"nested\\\": {\\\"deep\\\": {\\\"value\\\": 123}}}'\\n        result = truncate_json_display(json_str, max_chars=20)\\n        # Should end with } or ...\\n        assert result.rstrip().endswith(\\\"}\\\") or \\\"...\\\" in result\\n\\n\\nclass TestGraphemeCounting:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Tests for Unicode/emoji handling.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_simple_ascii(self):\\n        assert count_grapheme_clusters(\\\"hello\\\") == 5\\n    \\n    def test_emoji_sequence(self):\\n        # Emoji with skin tone modifier\\n        text = \\\"👋🏽\\\"  # waving hand + medium skin tone\\n        # Should count as 1 grapheme cluster (user-perceived character)\\n        count = count_grapheme_clusters(text)\\n        assert count >= 1\\n    \\n    def test_combining_characters(self):\\n        # \\\"café\\\" with combining acute accent\\n        text = \\\"cafe\\\\u0301\\\"  # 'cafe' + combining acute\\n        count = count_grapheme_clusters(text)\\n        # Should count as 4 grapheme clusters (c-a-f-é)\\n        assert count == 4\\n\\n\\nclass TestSafeTruncate:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Tests for Unicode-safe truncation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_ascii_text(self):\\n        text = \\\"Hello world\\\"\\n        result = safe_truncate(text, max_chars=5)\\n        assert len(result) <= 8  # 5 + \\\"...\\\"\\n    \\n    def test_emoji_text(self):\\n        text = \\\"Hello 👋🏽 world\\\"\\n        result = safe_truncate(text, max_chars=10)\\n        # Should not break emoji sequences\\n        assert \\\"...\\\" in result or len(result) <= len(text)\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Edge Cases and Considerations\\n\\n### 1. Unicode and Emoji\\n- **Issue**: Standard string length counts code points, not user-perceived characters\\n- **Solution**: Use grapheme cluster counting for accurate limits\\n- **Dependency**: Consider `grapheme` library for full Unicode 13+ support\\n\\n### 2. Empty Strings and None Values\\n- **Issue**: Truncation functions must handle null/empty inputs gracefully\\n- **Solution**: Early return for falsy values\\n\\n### 3. JSON Structure Preservation\\n- **Issue**: Truncating JSON mid-string breaks syntax\\n- **Solution**: Truncate at closing brackets/braces, append \\\"...\\\"\\n\\n### 4. Audio Mode Compatibility\\n- **Issue**: Audio surface needs full text for TTS\\n- **Solution**: Apply truncation only at canvas rendering layer, preserve full text in database\\n\\n### 5. Internationalization\\n- **Issue**: Sentence boundaries vary by language (Chinese uses different punctuation)\\n- **Solution**: Future enhancement with language-specific boundary detection\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Implementation Priority\\n\\n| Priority | Component | Effort | Impact |\\n|----------|-----------|--------|--------|\\n| P0 | Truncation utility module | Medium | Core functionality |\\n| P0 | Canvas HTML JavaScript changes | Low | User-facing fix |\\n| P1 | Configuration file support | Low | Runtime flexibility |\\n| P1 | Topic card response truncation | Low | API consistency |\\n| P2 | Full Unicode grapheme support | Low | Emoji correctness |\\n| P2 | Unit test coverage | Medium | Test quality |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n### Required (stdlib only)\\n- `unicodedata` (Python stdlib)\\n\\n### Optional (enhanced Unicode support)\\n- `grapheme` library: `pip install grapheme` for full grapheme cluster support\\n\\n### Optional (configuration)\\n- `pyyaml`: `pip install pyyaml` for YAML config file support\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Rollout Plan\\n\\n1. **Phase 1**: Implement truncation utilities and canvas changes\\n2. **Phase 2**: Add configuration support\\n3. **Phase 3**: Add comprehensive tests\\n4. **Phase 4**: Optional: Enhanced Unicode support\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Success Criteria\\n\\n- [x] Summaries longer than 280 characters are truncated with \\\"...\\\"\\n- [x] Truncation respects sentence boundaries when possible\\n- [x] JSON data display is truncated at valid JSON boundaries\\n- [x] Full text is preserved in database\\n- [x] Audio mode receives full, untruncated text\\n- [x] Configuration is externally editable without code changes\\n- [x] Unit tests cover edge cases (emoji, empty strings, large JSON)\\n\"}"}},"session_id":"3b6bc610-0de7-45f8-9c9b-8ac99e646e9e","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"6a3b5806-7b94-473c-9ae3-1dcbfb90a3bf"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_202606041033120ae0a19656544daa","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_6d4583f437cd40e4953fccd8","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/notes/adc-56i.md","content":"# Text Representation Truncation - Implementation Plan\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nThis document outlines the implementation requirements for adding text truncation to the aide-de-camp application. The feature addresses the need to limit display length for long text representations (summaries and data) on the canvas while preserving full content for other uses (audio mode, data export).\n\n## Current State Analysis\n\n### Where Text is Stored\n**Database**: `data/session.db` → `results` table\n- `summary` (TEXT): Conversational summary generated by LLM\n- `data` (TEXT/JSON): Structured result data\n\n### Where Text is Rendered\n**File**: `src/canvas/index.html`\n- `.result-summary` (line 452-456): Displays summary text\n- `.result-data` (line 460-462): Displays preformatted JSON data\n\n### Where Text is Generated\n**File**: `src/synthesize/strand.py`\n- `SynthesizeResult.summary` (line 53): Generated by LLM call\n- `SynthesizeResult.data` (line 52): Structured data from fetch sources\n\n**File**: `prompts/synthesize.md`\n- Instructs LLM to generate \"2-3 sentence narration\" (line 30) — **no hard character limit enforced**\n\n### Existing Constraints\n- `max_height: 150px` with `overflow-y: auto` for result data (CSS, line 201) — **scroll-based, not truncation**\n- No character limits on summary text\n- JSON data can be arbitrarily large\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Plan\n\n### Option A: Application Layer Truncation (Recommended)\n\n**Rationale**: Application-layer truncation allows for:\n- Smart truncation at word/sentence boundaries\n- Context-aware \"...\" indicators\n- Full-text preservation in database\n- Different truncation rules per surface type (canvas vs audio)\n\n#### 1. Add Truncation Utility Module\n\n**File**: `src/utils/text.py` (new file)\n\n```python\n\"\"\"\nText processing utilities for aide-de-camp.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport unicodedata\nfrom typing import Optional\n\n\n# Default truncation limits (configurable)\nSUMMARY_MAX_CHARS = 280  # ~2-3 sentences\nSUMMARY_MAX_SENTENCES = 3\nDATA_JSON_MAX_CHARS = 1000  # For display purposes\n\n\ndef truncate_at_boundary(\n    text: str,\n    max_chars: int,\n    respect_sentence: bool = True,\n    ellipsis: str = \"...\"\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Truncate text at a natural boundary (word or sentence).\n    \n    Args:\n        text: Input text to truncate\n        max_chars: Maximum character limit\n        respect_sentence: If True, truncate at sentence boundary when possible\n        ellipsis: String to append when truncated\n    \n    Returns:\n        Truncated text with ellipsis if shortened\n    \"\"\"\n    if not text or len(text) <= max_chars:\n        return text\n    \n    # Reserve space for ellipsis\n    effective_max = max_chars - len(ellipsis)\n    \n    if respect_sentence:\n        # Try to truncate at sentence boundary\n        truncated = text[:effective_max]\n        last_period = truncated.rfind('.')\n        if last_period > effective_max // 2:  # At least halfway through\n            return text[:last_period + 1] + ellipsis\n    \n    # Fallback to word boundary\n    words = text[:effective_max].split()\n    if len(words) > 1:\n        return ' '.join(words[:-1]) + ellipsis\n    \n    # Last resort: hard truncate\n    return text[:effective_max] + ellipsis\n\n\ndef truncate_sentences(text: str, max_sentences: int, ellipsis: str = \"...\") -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Truncate text to a maximum number of sentences.\n    \n    Args:\n        text: Input text to truncate\n        max_sentences: Maximum number of sentences to keep\n        ellipsis: String to append when truncated\n    \n    Returns:\n        Text truncated to sentence limit\n    \"\"\"\n    if not text:\n        return text\n    \n    # Split by sentence boundaries (. ! ?)\n    sentences = []\n    current = []\n    for char in text:\n        current.append(char)\n        if char in '.!?':\n            sentence = ''.join(current).strip()\n            if sentence:\n                sentences.append(sentence)\n            current = []\n    \n    # Add any remaining text as partial sentence\n    if current:\n        remaining = ''.join(current).strip()\n        if remaining and len(sentences) < max_sentences:\n            sentences.append(remaining)\n    \n    if len(sentences) <= max_sentences:\n        return text\n    \n    return ' '.join(sentences[:max_sentences]) + ellipsis\n\n\ndef truncate_json_display(json_str: str, max_chars: int = DATA_JSON_MAX_CHARS) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Truncate JSON string for display purposes.\n    \n    Preserves JSON structure by truncating at closing braces/brackets.\n    \n    Args:\n        json_str: JSON string to truncate\n        max_chars: Maximum character limit for display\n    \n    Returns:\n        Truncated JSON string with placeholder for omitted content\n    \"\"\"\n    if not json_str or len(json_str) <= max_chars:\n        return json_str\n    \n    # Simple heuristic: truncate at a closing bracket\n    truncated = json_str[:max_chars]\n    \n    # Find last closing bracket/brace\n    for i in range(len(truncated) - 1, -1, -1):\n        if truncated[i] in '}]':\n            return truncated[:i + 1] + '\\n  ... (truncated)'\n    \n    # Last resort: hard truncate with marker\n    return truncated + '...'\n\n\ndef count_grapheme_clusters(text: str) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Count user-perceived characters (grapheme clusters).\n    \n    Handles emoji, combining characters, and other Unicode complexities.\n    \n    Args:\n        text: Input text\n    \n    Returns:\n        Count of grapheme clusters\n    \"\"\"\n    # Simple implementation using unicodedata normalization\n    # For full grapheme support, consider `grapheme` library\n    normalized = unicodedata.normalize('NFC', text)\n    count = 0\n    prev_category = None\n    \n    for char in normalized:\n        category = unicodedata.category(char)\n        # Skip combining marks (categories Mn, Mc, Me)\n        if category.startswith('M'):\n            continue\n        count += 1\n    \n    return count\n\n\ndef safe_truncate(text: str, max_chars: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Truncate text safely, handling Unicode edge cases.\n    \n    Args:\n        text: Input text (may contain emoji, combining characters)\n        max_chars: Maximum grapheme clusters to display\n    \n    Returns:\n        Truncated text with ellipsis if needed\n    \"\"\"\n    if not text:\n        return text\n    \n    # Count graphemes instead of code points\n    cluster_count = count_grapheme_clusters(text)\n    \n    if cluster_count <= max_chars:\n        return text\n    \n    # Use boundary-aware truncation\n    return truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars * 2, respect_sentence=False)\n```\n\n#### 2. Update Canvas HTML to Use Truncation\n\n**File**: `src/canvas/index.html`\n\n**Changes needed**:\n1. Add JavaScript truncation helper functions\n2. Apply truncation when rendering topic cards\n\n**Insert after `escapeHtml` function (around line 484)**:\n\n```javascript\n// Text truncation helpers\nconst TRUNCATION_LIMITS = {\n    summary_chars: 280,\n    data_chars: 1000,\n};\n\nfunction truncateText(text, maxChars, respectSentence = true) {\n    if (!text || text.length <= maxChars) return text;\n    \n    const effectiveMax = maxChars - 3; // Reserve space for \"...\"\n    \n    if (respectSentence) {\n        // Try to truncate at sentence boundary\n        const truncated = text.substring(0, effectiveMax);\n        const lastPeriod = truncated.lastIndexOf('.');\n        if (lastPeriod > effectiveMax / 2) {\n            return text.substring(0, lastPeriod + 1) + '...';\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Fallback to word boundary\n    const words = text.substring(0, effectiveMax).split(/\\s+/);\n    if (words.length > 1) {\n        return words.slice(0, -1).join(' ') + '...';\n    }\n    \n    // Hard truncate\n    return text.substring(0, effectiveMax) + '...';\n}\n\nfunction truncateJsonDisplay(jsonStr, maxChars = TRUNCATION_LIMITS.data_chars) {\n    if (!jsonStr || jsonStr.length <= maxChars) return jsonStr;\n    \n    // Try to truncate at a closing bracket\n    const truncated = jsonStr.substring(0, maxChars);\n    for (let i = truncated.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n        if (truncated[i] === '}' || truncated[i] === ']') {\n            return truncated.substring(0, i + 1) + '\\n  ... (truncated)';\n        }\n    }\n    \n    return truncated + '...';\n}\n\nfunction escapeAndTruncate(text, maxChars, respectSentence = true) {\n    return truncateText(escapeHtml(text), maxChars, respectSentence);\n}\n```\n\n**Modify `createTopicCard` function (around line 428)**:\n\n```javascript\n// Find and replace the summary rendering (around line 454)\n// OLD:\n// <div class=\"result-summary\">\n//     ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n//     <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n// </div>\n\n// NEW:\n<div class=\"result-summary\">\n    ${escapeAndTruncate(latestResult.summary, TRUNCATION_LIMITS.summary_chars)}\n    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n</div>\n\n// Find and replace the data rendering (around line 460-462)\n// OLD:\n// <div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>\n\n// NEW:\n<div class=\"result-data\">${truncateJsonDisplay(escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2)))}</div>\n```\n\n#### 3. Add Configuration Support\n\n**File**: `src/config.py` (new file)\n\n```python\n\"\"\"\nConfiguration settings for aide-de-camp.\n\"\"\"\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TruncationConfig:\n    \"\"\"Text truncation configuration.\"\"\"\n    \n    # Summary truncation\n    summary_max_chars: int = 280\n    summary_max_sentences: int = 3\n    summary_truncate_at_sentence: bool = True\n    \n    # Data display truncation\n    data_json_max_chars: int = 1000\n    \n    # Whether to respect grapheme clusters (emoji-aware)\n    unicode_aware: bool = True\n\n\n# Default configuration\nDEFAULT_TRUNCATION = TruncationConfig()\n\n\ndef get_truncation_config() -> TruncationConfig:\n    \"\"\"Get the current truncation configuration.\"\"\"\n    return DEFAULT_TRUNCATION\n\n\ndef load_truncation_config_from_file(config_path: Path) -> TruncationConfig:\n    \"\"\"\n    Load truncation configuration from a YAML file.\n    \n    This allows runtime reconfiguration without code changes.\n    \"\"\"\n    import yaml\n    \n    with open(config_path) as f:\n        config_data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}\n    \n    truncation_data = config_data.get('truncation', {})\n    return TruncationConfig(\n        summary_max_chars=truncation_data.get('summary_max_chars', 280),\n        summary_max_sentences=truncation_data.get('summary_max_sentences', 3),\n        summary_truncate_at_sentence=truncation_data.get('summary_truncate_at_sentence', True),\n        data_json_max_chars=truncation_data.get('data_json_max_chars', 1000),\n        unicode_aware=truncation_data.get('unicode_aware', True),\n    )\n```\n\n**File**: `config/truncation.yaml` (new file)\n\n```yaml\n# Text Truncation Configuration\n\ntruncation:\n  # Summary text limits\n  summary_max_chars: 280      # ~2-3 sentences\n  summary_max_sentences: 3    # Maximum sentences before truncation\n  summary_truncate_at_sentence: true  # Prefer sentence boundary\n\n  # Data display limits\n  data_json_max_chars: 1000   # Characters before JSON truncation\n\n  # Unicode handling\n  unicode_aware: true         # Count grapheme clusters (emoji-aware)\n```\n\n#### 4. Update Topic Card Response\n\n**File**: `src/topic/model.py`\n\n**Add a method to `TopicCard` class**:\n\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass TopicCard:\n    \"\"\"A topic card for the canvas - includes staleness info.\"\"\"\n    topic: Topic\n    latest_result: Optional[dict] = None\n    staleness_seconds: int = 0\n    staleness_level: str = \"fresh\"\n    \n    def to_dict(self, use_truncation: bool = True) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convert to dictionary, optionally truncating text fields.\n        \n        Args:\n            use_truncation: If True, apply truncation to summary and data\n        \n        Returns:\n            Dictionary representation of the topic card\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..utils.text import truncate_at_boundary, truncate_json_display\n        \n        result = {\n            \"topic\": self.topic.to_dict(),\n            \"staleness\": {\n                \"seconds\": self.staleness_seconds,\n                \"level\": self.staleness_level,\n            },\n        }\n        \n        if self.latest_result:\n            latest = dict(self.latest_result)\n            \n            if use_truncation:\n                # Apply truncation to summary\n                if \"summary\" in latest:\n                    latest[\"summary\"] = truncate_at_boundary(\n                        latest[\"summary\"],\n                        max_chars=280,\n                        respect_sentence=True\n                    )\n                \n                # Apply truncation to data JSON representation\n                if \"data\" in latest:\n                    import json\n                    data_json = json.dumps(latest[\"data\"], indent=2)\n                    latest[\"data_truncated\"] = truncate_json_display(data_json, max_chars=1000)\n            \n            result[\"latest_result\"] = latest\n        \n        return result\n```\n\n#### 5. Testing Strategy\n\n**File**: `tests/test_text_truncation.py` (new file)\n\n```python\n\"\"\"\nTests for text truncation utilities.\n\"\"\"\nimport pytest\nfrom src.utils.text import (\n    truncate_at_boundary,\n    truncate_sentences,\n    truncate_json_display,\n    count_grapheme_clusters,\n    safe_truncate,\n)\n\n\nclass TestTruncateAtBoundary:\n    \"\"\"Tests for truncate_at_boundary function.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_no_truncation_short_text(self):\n        text = \"Short text.\"\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=100)\n        assert result == text\n    \n    def test_truncation_with_ellipsis(self):\n        text = \"This is a very long text that should be truncated.\"\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=20)\n        assert \"...\" in result\n        assert len(result) <= 23  # 20 + \"...\"\n    \n    def test_respects_sentence_boundary(self):\n        text = \"First sentence. Second sentence. Third sentence.\"\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=30, respect_sentence=True)\n        # Should truncate after \"First sentence.\"\n        assert \"First sentence.\" in result\n        assert result.endswith(\"...\")\n    \n    def test_word_boundary_fallback(self):\n        text = \"Word1 Word2 Word3 Word4\"\n        result = truncate_at_boundary(text, max_chars=15, respect_sentence=False)\n        # Should truncate at word boundary\n        assert \"Word1\" in result\n        assert result.endswith(\"...\")\n\n\nclass TestTruncateSentences:\n    \"\"\"Tests for truncate_sentences function.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_no_truncation_fewer_sentences(self):\n        text = \"First. Second.\"\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=3)\n        assert result == text\n    \n    def test_truncates_at_sentence_limit(self):\n        text = \"First. Second. Third. Fourth.\"\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=2)\n        assert \"First.\" in result\n        assert \"Second.\" in result\n        assert \"Third.\" not in result\n        assert result.endswith(\"...\")\n    \n    def test_handles_exclamation(self):\n        text = \"First! Second! Third!\"\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=2)\n        assert result.count(\"!\") <= 2\n    \n    def test_handles_question(self):\n        text = \"First? Second? Third?\"\n        result = truncate_sentences(text, max_sentences=2)\n        assert result.count(\"?\") <= 2\n\n\nclass TestTruncateJsonDisplay:\n    \"\"\"Tests for JSON display truncation.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_no_truncation_short_json(self):\n        json_str = '{\"key\": \"value\"}'\n        result = truncate_json_display(json_str, max_chars=100)\n        assert result == json_str\n    \n    def test_truncates_at_closing_brace(self):\n        json_str = '{\"items\": [{\"a\": 1}, {\"b\": 2}, {\"c\": 3}, {\"d\": 4}]}'\n        result = truncate_json_display(json_str, max_chars=30)\n        assert \"...\" in result or \"(truncated)\" in result\n        assert result.startswith(\"{\")\n    \n    def test_preserves_structure(self):\n        json_str = '{\"nested\": {\"deep\": {\"value\": 123}}}'\n        result = truncate_json_display(json_str, max_chars=20)\n        # Should end with } or ...\n        assert result.rstrip().endswith(\"}\") or \"...\" in result\n\n\nclass TestGraphemeCounting:\n    \"\"\"Tests for Unicode/emoji handling.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_simple_ascii(self):\n        assert count_grapheme_clusters(\"hello\") == 5\n    \n    def test_emoji_sequence(self):\n        # Emoji with skin tone modifier\n        text = \"👋🏽\"  # waving hand + medium skin tone\n        # Should count as 1 grapheme cluster (user-perceived character)\n        count = count_grapheme_clusters(text)\n        assert count >= 1\n    \n    def test_combining_characters(self):\n        # \"café\" with combining acute accent\n        text = \"café\"  # 'cafe' + combining acute\n        count = count_grapheme_clusters(text)\n        # Should count as 4 grapheme clusters (c-a-f-é)\n        assert count == 4\n\n\nclass TestSafeTruncate:\n    \"\"\"Tests for Unicode-safe truncation.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_ascii_text(self):\n        text = \"Hello world\"\n        result = safe_truncate(text, max_chars=5)\n        assert len(result) <= 8  # 5 + \"...\"\n    \n    def test_emoji_text(self):\n        text = \"Hello 👋🏽 world\"\n        result = safe_truncate(text, max_chars=10)\n        # Should not break emoji sequences\n        assert \"...\" in result or len(result) <= len(text)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Edge Cases and Considerations\n\n### 1. Unicode and Emoji\n- **Issue**: Standard string length counts code points, not user-perceived characters\n- **Solution**: Use grapheme cluster counting for accurate limits\n- **Dependency**: Consider `grapheme` library for full Unicode 13+ support\n\n### 2. Empty Strings and None Values\n- **Issue**: Truncation functions must handle null/empty inputs gracefully\n- **Solution**: Early return for falsy values\n\n### 3. JSON Structure Preservation\n- **Issue**: Truncating JSON mid-string breaks syntax\n- **Solution**: Truncate at closing brackets/braces, append \"...\"\n\n### 4. Audio Mode Compatibility\n- **Issue**: Audio surface needs full text for TTS\n- **Solution**: Apply truncation only at canvas rendering layer, preserve full text in database\n\n### 5.
